title: SAML Single Sign-On (SSO)
description: SAML 2.0 based single sign-on for enterprise identity provider integration; enables centralized authentication with Okta, Azure AD, or other IdPs.

cost.costNotes: SAML SSO has moderate infrastructure costs and typically requires enterprise IdP subscriptions.

provenance.estimatedMonthlyRangeUsd: SAML SSO infrastructure costs vary by IdP choice. Min=$0 for self-hosted open-source SAML SP implementation (passport-saml, Spring Security SAML, SimpleSAMLphp) with no IdP costs if customer brings their own IdP. Max=$3000 represents production enterprise scenarios: (1) Enterprise IdP subscription if providing SSO - Okta Workforce Identity ($2-8/user/mo for 50-500 users = $100-4000/mo), Azure AD Premium P1 ($6/user/mo), Ping Identity ($3-10/user/mo), (2) SAML service provider infrastructure for high availability - load balancer ($30/mo), application servers ($100-200/mo), session storage Redis ($30-50/mo), certificate management ($0-50/mo), (3) Monitoring and logging ($50-100/mo). B2B SaaS apps typically do NOT pay IdP costs (customer provides their own Okta/Azure AD), so costs are primarily infrastructure ($200-500/mo). Apps providing SSO to end users bear full IdP costs. Auth0 B2B SSO starts at $240/mo base + per-connection fees.
provenance.adoptionCost: High complexity implementation. SAML SSO requires: (1) Understanding SAML 2.0 protocol (assertions, bindings, profiles, certificates) - 20-40 hours learning, (2) Service Provider (SP) implementation with SAML library integration - 40-60 hours development, (3) Certificate management and key rotation setup - 12-20 hours, (4) IdP integration and metadata exchange for each customer IdP (Okta, Azure AD, Google, Ping) - 8-16 hours per IdP, (5) Attribute mapping and user provisioning logic - 16-24 hours, (6) Session management post-authentication - 12-16 hours, (7) Testing across multiple IdPs with different configurations - 20-30 hours, (8) Support for SP-initiated and IdP-initiated flows - 12-20 hours, (9) Admin UI for SAML configuration - 20-30 hours, (10) Documentation and customer onboarding guides - 12-20 hours. Total: 172-276 developer hours at $150/hour = $25,800-41,400. With mature SAML libraries (passport-saml, python3-saml, OneLogin SAML toolkits), pre-built integration guides, and AI assistance reducing XML/certificate handling code by 40-50%, realistic adoption cost is $5000-8000 for teams with enterprise auth experience, representing 30-50 focused development hours plus extensive testing time.
provenance.licenseCost: SAML 2.0 is an open standard (OASIS) with no licensing fees. Service Provider implementation uses open-source libraries: passport-saml (Node.js), python3-saml/python-saml (Python), spring-security-saml (Java), ruby-saml (Ruby), SimpleSAMLphp (PHP) - all free. However, enterprise customers often require commercial IdP subscriptions: Okta, Azure AD Premium, Ping Identity, OneLogin charge per-user fees ($2-10/user/mo). For B2B SaaS, customers provide their own IdP at no cost to the service provider. Self-hosted IdP options (Keycloak, Shibboleth, SimpleSAMLphp) are open-source with optional commercial support contracts.

provides.reasoning: This pattern implements SAML 2.0 authentication protocol, enabling users to authenticate via enterprise identity providers. This is more specific than saml-sso as it clearly indicates the authentication mechanism.
This pattern provides SSO functionality, allowing users to authenticate once and access multiple applications. This is the core user experience capability delivered by the pattern.
SAML SSO inherently provides federated identity management by delegating authentication to external identity providers. This is a key capability that downstream systems may depend on.

requires.reasoning: SAML SSO absolutely requires an external identity provider (Okta, Azure AD, etc.) to function. The pattern cannot operate without an IdP to handle authentication assertions.

SAML relies on XML digital signatures and encryption, requiring X.509 certificates for secure assertion exchange between service provider and identity provider. This is a critical technical dependency.
After successful SAML authentication, the application needs session management to maintain authenticated state. This is required to track user sessions post-authentication.

availability.reason: General architectural pattern applicable broadly for enterprise applications

supports_nfr.reason: This pattern is exclusively for SAML 2.0 based authentication and is only applicable when SAML auth is required.
SAML SSO supports GDPR through federated identity management and audit trails but is compatible with both GDPR and non-GDPR applications.
SAML SSO supports HIPAA compliance through secure authentication and audit logging but works for both HIPAA and non-HIPAA applications.
SAML SSO supports CCPA through centralized identity management and user attribute control but is compatible with both CCPA and non-CCPA applications.
SAML SSO supports SOX compliance through centralized authentication and audit trails but works for both SOX and non-SOX applications.

supports_constraints.reason: SAML SSO is a protocol-level standard that works across all cloud providers, on-premises, and hybrid environments.
SAML SSO is supported by mature libraries in all major backend languages with XML processing capabilities.
SAML SSO relies on browser-based redirect flows requiring a human user interaction via web browser or embedded webview.
SAML SSO works independently of application caching strategies and is compatible with or without caching layers.
SAML SSO is a synchronous authentication mechanism that works regardless of whether the application uses async messaging.
SAML SSO provides authentication for any application type including those with AI inference workloads.
SAML SSO is commonly used in multi-tenant B2B SaaS where each customer uses their own IdP, but also works for single-tenant applications.
SAML SSO is compatible with applications that include batch processing workloads alongside interactive user authentication.
SAML SSO works independently of application transaction coordination requirements and is compatible with distributed transactions.
SAML SSO provides authentication for applications regardless of whether they include real-time streaming features.
SAML SSO authentication is independent of application data store capabilities including vector search functionality.
SAML SSO works with applications using any data store type including document-oriented databases.
SAML SSO is compatible with applications using key-value stores for session or data storage.
SAML SSO provides authentication independently of application database architecture including graph databases.
SAML SSO works with applications using time-series databases as authentication is independent of data storage type.
SAML SSO provides authentication for OLTP applications without impacting transactional workload characteristics.
SAML SSO is compatible with analytical workloads and provides authentication independently of OLAP query patterns.

flow_type.description: SAML flow support. sp_initiated (user starts at SP, redirected to IdP) is most secure, idp_initiated (user starts at IdP portal) is convenient but has security risks, both supports flexibility.

assertion_consumer_binding.description: SAML binding for assertion delivery. http_post is standard and supports larger payloads, http_redirect has URL length limits but simpler.

signature_algorithm.description: XML signature algorithm. rsa_sha256 is standard and widely supported, rsa_sha512 for higher security, ecdsa_sha256 for smaller signatures.

attribute_mapping_strategy.description: How to map SAML assertion attributes to user profile. configurable allows admin control, standard_claims uses common names (email, firstName, etc.), custom_per_idp handles IdP-specific attributes.

session_duration_minutes.description: Application session duration after SAML authentication. Shorter (60-240 min) improves security, longer (480-1440 min) reduces re-authentication frequency. 480 min (8 hours) is common for business apps.

require_signed_assertions.description: Require SAML assertions to be digitally signed by IdP. Strongly recommended for security - prevents assertion tampering.

require_encrypted_assertions.description: Require SAML assertions to be encrypted. Adds security for sensitive attributes but increases complexity. Optional - assertions are typically sent over HTTPS.

certificate_rotation_days.description: SP certificate rotation frequency. Shorter intervals (90-180 days) improve security but require coordination with IdPs. 365 days (1 year) is common balance.

requires_nfr.reason: SAML SSO availability is limited to 99.9% due to dependency on external IdP availability and network connectivity.
SAML SSO adds minimum 200ms p95 latency due to XML signature verification, IdP roundtrip, and assertion processing.
P99 latency accounts for full SAML flow including IdP interaction, occasional certificate validation delays, and network variability.
SAML SSO recovery requires IdP availability verification, metadata synchronization, and certificate validation restart.
Pattern supports up to 1M authentications/hour due to XML processing overhead, signature verification CPU cost, and IdP rate limits.

SAML SSO in enterprise contexts typically requires comprehensive audit logging for compliance, security monitoring, and forensic analysis. Without audit logging, organizations lose visibility into authentication events.

warn_nfr.reason: SAML SSO is a critical authentication gateway for enterprise applications. Availability targets below 99% indicate tolerance for significant downtime which conflicts with enterprise authentication expectations.
warn_nfr.message: sec-auth-saml-sso: availability target is {actual} (<99%). SAML SSO is a critical authentication dependency; low availability targets may impact user access during IdP outages.
